appTagline: Sanctions, country risk & circumvention analytics for customers and suppliers

search.placeholder: Search any of 200 customers or suppliers…

home.heroTitle: Risk score every customer and supplier
home.heroSub: Six-dimension assessment combining FATF, OFAC, UN, EU sanctions, Basel AML Index, Transparency International CPI, World Bank WGI, and OpenSanctions. Every factor links back to its source.
home.top10Sub: Sorted by overall composite score. Synthetic names for illustrative purposes.
home.geoSub: Average risk score by headquarters country.

company.illustrative: Illustrative placeholder — real company name used only to anchor a low-risk tier-1 profile.

companies.title: All companies
companies.sub: Full book of monitored customers and suppliers with live scores.

methodology.title: Scoring methodology
methodology.sub: Transparent, rule-based composite of six AML skills. No ML; every factor is deterministic and citable.
methodology.dataRealityTitle: Data reality — what's real vs. mock

exports.title: NVIDIA Export Regulation Reference
exports.sub: Full snapshot of NVIDIA's public parts classification — ECCN, HTS, TPP and lifecycle state for every published part. Scraped from NVIDIA's own ECCN portal and packaged as a downloadable spreadsheet.
exports.apiNote: Data via nvidia.com's public ECCN API (api-prod.nvidia.com/services/eccn/v1/getECCN)
exports.homeCardSub: {total} parts · ECCN + HTS classifications · downloadable Excel

sources.title: Data sources
sources.sub: Authoritative public sources used to score country-level factors. Snapshot dates reflect when we last refreshed the local data file.

disclaimer.short: Illustrative demo — synthetic company data, real country snapshots.
disclaimer.full: This application is a technical demonstration of AML/compliance scoring architecture. Company risk scores are generated from synthetic data. Real company names appearing in the demo are used only as benign placeholders to illustrate low-risk tier-1 entities and do not reflect any actual compliance assessment. Country-level reference data is sourced from public authorities with snapshot dates indicated on the Sources page and may be outdated relative to current lists. Not a substitute for professional sanctions screening or due diligence.
